title: Cross-cutting — TACACS+ Privileged Command Audit Coverage Across Networks

compliance.assurance_rationale: AU-2 event logging — privileged commands logged with full attribution.
A.8.2 privileged access rights — cross-cutting privileged-command coverage.
PCI 10.2.1.5 — actions taken by individuals with admin or root privileges logged.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263) + Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services (1915).
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (11042, 11043) earliest=-1d
| stats count, dc(user) as users, dc(device_ip) as devices by command_authz_result
| eval evidence_status="privileged_command_coverage"
| collect index=audit_evidence
description: We aggregate TACACS+ privileged command coverage across all networks for cross-cutting compliance evidence.
value: Multiple frameworks (NIST 800-53, ISO 27001, PCI DSS) require privileged-command logging. This UC produces a single coverage measurement that satisfies all of them.
implementation: Schedule the saved search daily at 06:00 local time; archive results to the `audit_evidence` summary index for the cross-cutting MFA-control evidence pack.
knownFalsePositives: 1. Approved planned changes — annotate with ticket reference.
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- UC-17.1.43 operational.

## Step 1-5

```ini
[UC-22.40.6: Privileged Command Coverage]
cron_schedule = 0 7 * * *
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.40.6,family=privileged_access
```
grandmaExplanation: We aggregate TACACS+ privileged command coverage across all networks for cross-cutting compliance evidence. That is important for the audit, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.
